seo.title: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Azure AI Agent Service Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix responsible ai governance for agents in Azure AI Agent Service agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Azure AI Agent Service, Responsible AI Governance for Agents fails when enterprise agents bypass policy validation on tool outputs, ingest unfiltered grounding data, or exceed token limits without safeguards. These issues manifest through unvalidated Bing Search results containing outdated information, file search tools returning Azure metadata instead of content, and thread token overflows causing erratic behavior. Without middleware governance, agents violate enterprise compliance while appearing functional.

howItManifests.scenario: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
howItManifests.symptom: Agents execute Bing Search and Bing File Search tools without filtering PII or sensitive data from results
howItManifests.impact: Compliance violations and data leakage to unauthorized users

howItManifests.scenario: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
howItManifests.symptom: Agents cite 2023 election results in 2025 responses from cached Bing data
howItManifests.impact: Hallucinations and loss of user trust in enterprise decision-making

howItManifests.scenario: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
howItManifests.symptom: Bing File Search returns SharePoint file paths and permissions instead of document content
howItManifests.impact: Incomplete context leads to incorrect agent reasoning and decisions

howItManifests.scenario: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
howItManifests.symptom: Long-running agent threads hit 128K token limit, causing truncation mid-response
howItManifests.impact: Lost conversation context and broken user experiences

detection.name: Detect Unvalidated Tool Outputs
detection.description: Intercepts Azure AI Agent Service tool calls to check for missing policy validation

detection.name: Detect Outdated Bing Grounding Data
detection.description: Scans agent responses for temporal inconsistencies in Bing-grounded content

detection.name: Detect Token Limit Exceeded Threads
detection.description: Monitors Azure AI Agent thread token usage against 128K limit
detection.code:
from azure.ai.projects.client import ProjectsClient

client = ProjectsClient(credential=DefaultAzureCredential())

project_name = 'your-project'
agent_id = 'your-agent-id'

threads = client.agents.list_threads(project_name=project_name, agent_id=agent_id)

for thread in threads:
    messages = client.agents.list_messages(project_name, agent_id, thread.id)
    total_tokens = sum(msg.usage.total_tokens for msg in messages if msg.usage)
    
    if total_tokens > 120000:  # Alert before 128K limit
        print(f'🚨 TOKEN LIMIT RISK: Thread {thread.id} = {total_tokens:,} tokens')
        if total_tokens > 128000:
            print('💥 THREAD EXCEEDED TOKEN LIMIT')

fixes.name: Policy Validation Middleware
fixes.description: Intercepts tool calls with enterprise policy filtering before execution
fixes.code:
from vex_sdk import VexMiddleware
from azure.ai.projects import AgentExtension

class ResponsibleAIMiddleware(VexMiddleware):
    def __init__(self):
        self.policies = ['no_pii', 'no_financial_data', 'content_moderation']
    
    async def pre_tool_call(self, tool_call, context):
        tool_name = tool_call.function.name
        
        if tool_name in ['bing_search', 'bing_file_search']:
            # Apply enterprise policies
            validated_input = self.validate_query(tool_call.function.arguments.query)
            tool_call.function.arguments.query = validated_input
            
        return tool_call
    
    def validate_query(self, query):
        # Mock enterprise policy check
        if 'ssn' in query.lower() or 'credit card' in query.lower():
            raise ValueError('Policy violation: PII detected')
        return query

# Register with Azure AI Agent Service
middleware = ResponsibleAIMiddleware()
agent_extension = AgentExtension(middleware=middleware)

fixes.name: Freshness-Aware Grounding
fixes.description: Filters Bing results by recency and validates temporal accuracy

fixes.name: Graceful Token Limit Handler
fixes.description: Implements thread summarization and context rotation before token limits
fixes.code:
from azure.ai.projects.client import ProjectsClient

class TokenManager:
    MAX_TOKENS = 110000  # Conservative limit
    
    async def check_and_rotate_thread(self, client, project_name, agent_id, thread_id):
        messages = client.agents.list_messages(project_name, agent_id, thread_id)
        total_tokens = sum(msg.usage.total_tokens for msg in messages if msg.usage)
        
        if total_tokens > self.MAX_TOKENS:
            summary = await self.summarize_thread(messages[-20:])  # Last 20 messages
            
            # Create new thread with summary as first message
            new_thread = client.agents.create_thread(
                project_name=project_name,
                agent_id=agent_id,
                messages=[{'role': 'system', 'content': f'CONTEXT SUMMARY: {summary}'}]
            )
            
            print(f'🔄 Rotated to new thread: {new_thread.id}')
            return new_thread.id
        
    async def summarize_thread(self, messages):
        # Use Azure OpenAI for summarization
        summary_prompt = 'Summarize this conversation for continuity: ' + '\n'.join([m.content for m in messages])
        return await call_azure_openai(summary_prompt)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Responsible AI Governance for Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as middleware in the Azure AI Agent Service processing pipeline, automatically intercepting all tool calls, grounding data, and thread operations. It applies enterprise policies in real-time, validates Bing search freshness, filters file metadata, and manages token limits with zero-code configuration. Vex provides observability dashboards showing compliance rates, token usage trends, and policy violation alerts across all agents.

The Vex SDK (`pip install azure-ai-projects vex-sdk`) creates a governance layer that works with any Azure AI Agent without code changes. It automatically detects the four common failure modes and applies remediations using your enterprise policy definitions stored in Azure. Vex also generates compliance reports for audit trails, making Responsible AI governance continuous rather than manual.

Integration takes 5 minutes: install the SDK, configure your Azure AI Project credentials, define policies in Vex dashboard, and enable the middleware. Vex then monitors 100% of agent interactions with sub-second latency overhead.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk.azure import VexAzureAgentGuard
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

# 1. Initialize Vex Guard for Azure AI Agent Service
vex_guard = VexAzureAgentGuard(
    subscription_id='your-sub-id',
    resource_group='your-rg',
    policies={
        'no_pii': True,
        'freshness_days': 90,
        'max_tokens': 110000,
        'file_content_only': True
    }
)

# 2. Protect your agent deployment
@vex_guard.protect_agent(project_name='your-project', agent_id='your-agent')
def enterprise_agent_handler(messages):
    return azure_ai_agent_service.run(messages)

# 3. Vex auto-detects and fixes all 4 failure modes
# Real-time alerts to Azure Monitor + compliance dashboard

# Deploy with one command
vex_guard.deploy_to_azure_ai_project('your-project')

print('✅ Vex Responsible AI Governance enabled')

# View compliance
print(vex_guard.compliance_report())
